articles.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
articles.summary: Granola, an AI note-taking app, makes notes viewable to anyone with a link by default and uses them for AI training unless users opt out, despite claiming privacy protections.

articles.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
articles.summary: Elon Musk is expected to file for SpaceX's IPO, with the earliest potential date in June following an April filing and SEC review process.

articles.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a popular daily livestream talk show featuring interviews with AI executives and tech leaders. The show broadcasts weekdays on X and YouTube, competing with major financial news networks.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a popular Silicon Valley tech podcast, which will remain independent under Chris Lehane's oversight as chief political operative.

articles.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
articles.summary: Microsoft released three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text, generating audio, and creating images, six months after the group's formation.

articles.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
articles.summary: Google's Vids app now enables users to customize and direct avatars through text prompts for video creation, enhancing the app's video generation capabilities.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud, capitalizing on developer frustration with legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical functionality as a free, open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring coded AI tokens. The startup's creative puzzle challenge attracted thousands of talented engineers and gained significant investor attention.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Novel Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs enable malicious users in cloud environments to gain full root control of shared machines by exploiting DRAM memory bit flip vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Quantum computers require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic-curve encryption, with new research showing potential to crack 256-bit ECC in days using 100x less overhead.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google accelerated its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current encryption. The company urges global adoption of post-quantum cryptography to protect sensitive data.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict are impacting petrochemical supply chains, threatening to increase plastic costs globally. Americans may soon feel economic ripple effects across plastic product prices.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: The Iran conflict has driven fuel prices to $4+ per gallon in the US. Plastic shortages may follow, as petrochemical supply chains face disruption from the oil bottleneck.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers worldwide, like a Nigerian medical student, record themselves performing daily tasks to train humanoid robots. Companies like Micro1 collect this video data from thousands of workers across 50+ countries to advance robotics development.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, an advanced multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device. The model represents frontier-level intelligence capabilities with improved performance across text and image understanding.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use technology, advancing AI's ability to interact with digital interfaces. This innovation pushes the boundaries of what artificial intelligence can accomplish in practical computing tasks.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Unable to provide summary - no article content provided. Please share the article text for analysis.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field at Microsoft.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology more accessible for developers and farmers to build sustainable farming solutions.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduced Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance cost efficiency with latency performance.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 models, enabling high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more fluid, natural voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI applications.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family while discussion covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate new visual details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: Kilo launched KiloClaw to govern autonomous agents and manage shadow AI deployments. The platform addresses employees bypassing procurement to deploy agents on personal infrastructure, exposing enterprise data.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered defense strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Strict access governance and encryption are foundational practices to secure AI systems.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology, targeting development of high-performance AI chips, software, and new model architectures through 2030.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and reliability of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and pathfinding challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets OpenClaw AI agents running on Mac Minis, exploiting macOS binaries to harvest credentials as developers deploy local AI agents.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enabled an LLM to function as a theoretical collaborator, producing a testable multiverse model for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Arcee AI Releases Trinity Large Thinking: An Apache 2.0 Open Reasoning Model for Long-Horizon Agents and Tool Use
articles.summary: Arcee AI released Trinity Large Thinking, an open-source reasoning model under Apache 2.0 license designed for complex multi-step reasoning, long-horizon agents, and tool use.

articles.title: Defeating the ‘Token Tax’: How Google Gemma 4, NVIDIA, and OpenClaw are Revolutionizing Local Agentic AI: From RTX Desktops to DGX Spark
articles.summary: Google Gemma 4 and NVIDIA hardware enable local AI deployment without cloud token costs. Developers can now build personalized AI agents on RTX desktops and edge devices using platforms like OpenClaw.

articles.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 3B Vision: A New Vision Language Model for Enterprise Grade Document Data Extraction
articles.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a specialized vision-language model for enterprise document data extraction. It uses a modular adapter approach to enable accurate structured data conversion from complex visual documents.

articles.title: Brick vs. Bloom Card: I tested both for my screen addiction, and the winner depends on you
articles.summary: Brick and Bloom Card are both app-blocking tools for screen addiction. Brick offers a cheaper alternative with unique features, though each has tradeoffs depending on user needs.

articles.title: I turned to PrivacyBee to clean up my data - here's how it made me disappear
articles.summary: PrivacyBee is a comprehensive data removal service that effectively eliminates personal information across hundreds of websites, helping users protect their privacy online.

articles.title: How to switch from ChatGPT to Gemini - without starting from scratch
articles.summary: Gemini now allows users to transfer memories, chat history, and preferences from other AI assistants, eliminating the need to restart when switching platforms.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are moving beyond dashboards into more visible applications. Key trends to watch in 2026 include emerging developments in ML technology and deployment.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems use state-managed interruptions to halt agent execution pipelines, enabling human oversight. This human-in-the-loop approach provides approval gates for autonomous agent actions.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference mechanics: prefill attention processing, decode phase for token generation, and KV cache optimization techniques for efficient inference.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: This article explains linear regression through the lens of vector projections, demonstrating how least squares can be understood as a geometric projection problem that leads to accurate predictions.

articles.title: How to Handle Classical Data in Quantum Models
articles.summary: The article explores workflows and encoding techniques for integrating classical data into quantum machine learning models, enabling hybrid quantum-classical computing approaches.

articles.title: Quantum Simulations with Python
articles.summary: This article explores running quantum experiments using Qiskit-Aer, a Python-based quantum simulation tool that enables developers to test quantum algorithms and circuits.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, fast models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy title.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that operate as intelligent grid assets, enabling efficient power management and faster grid integration while improving system reliability.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions beyond single-turn exchanges. The framework systematically assesses agent performance on helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore allows controlling AI agent internet access through AWS Network Firewall domain filtering when deployed in a VPC, preventing unauthorized website access and data exfiltration while blocking malicious domains.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's VC-6 batch mode accelerates vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match improving model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models launch, designed to scale from data centers to edge devices like NVIDIA Jetson. These models address growing demand for diverse deployment scenarios.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables single-digit microsecond latency AI inference for algorithmic trading, allowing firms to compete with high-speed electronic markets using advanced neural network models instead of traditional specialized hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative XR technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in immersive computing.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understanding of how these complex systems make decisions, advancing AI transparency and safety.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an information estimator to quantify measurement quality. The approach maps objects through optical systems and analyzes how noise affects image distinction capabilities.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
